title: mattiastrum trinervium
intro: Mattiastrum trinervium, also known as the three-veined mattiastrum, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is known to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as having potential antiviral and antibacterial effects. Mattiastrum trinervium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for use in herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
